I've used 29 of about 29 target actions. I need to be more efficient. Let me think about the pattern:
1. SPACE transforms the scene
2. Moving in a direction moves the character
3. When the character is in a certain position relative to the green frame, pressing SPACE converts red blocks to gray
Looking at the current image, I see red blocks on the left side. This suggests that the previous action didn't convert them to gray. Let me check the current state to understand the pattern better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("=== Current positions ===")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Region (rows 30-52, cols 32-55) ===")
for i in range(30, 52):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][32:55]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
